title: Michael Saylor Criticizes Bitcoin's BIP-110 Proposal as 'Bad Idea'
summary: Michael Saylor, a prominent Bitcoin advocate, opposes BIP-110, a proposal to block spam data from the Bitcoin blockchain. He argues it undermines the network's neutrality and could lead to censorship.
body:
Michael Saylor, the executive chairman of MicroStrategy and a vocal Bitcoin advocate, has publicly criticized a new proposal aimed at cleaning up the Bitcoin blockchain. The proposal, known as BIP-110, suggests temporarily blocking "spam" data from the network to improve efficiency and reduce clutter.

Saylor argues that this approach would undermine Bitcoin's core principle of neutrality. He believes that allowing any form of censorship, even if targeted at spam, sets a dangerous precedent. "The idea of selectively blocking data goes against the fundamental ethos of Bitcoin, which is to be an open and permissionless network," Saylor stated in a recent interview.

The debate over BIP-110 highlights a broader tension within the Bitcoin community. While some supporters advocate for measures to enhance the network's efficiency, others, like Saylor, prioritize maintaining Bitcoin's decentralized and uncensored nature. For everyday users, this discussion is crucial as it could impact the future accessibility and functionality of the Bitcoin network.

As the debate continues, it's important for users to stay informed about potential changes to the Bitcoin protocol. Keeping an eye on developments from key stakeholders like Saylor and the Bitcoin Core developers will be essential in understanding how these discussions might shape the future of Bitcoin.
twitterThread: Michael Saylor calls BIP-110, a proposal to block 'spam' data on Bitcoin, a 'bad idea' that could enable censorship. 🚨
Saylor argues that selectively blocking data undermines Bitcoin's neutrality, a core principle of the network. 🔍

For everyday users, this debate could impact Bitcoin's future. Stay tuned as the discussion unfolds. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-19-michael-saylor-criticizes-bitcoins-bip-110-proposal-as-bad-idea
standaloneTweet: Michael Saylor warns that BIP-110, a proposal to block spam on Bitcoin, could enable censorship and undermine the network's neutrality. 🚨
